is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study investigates mutations in DHFR gene of Plasmodium falciparum; inclusion criterion was monoinfection with P. falciparum.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports Pfdhfr codons 51 (asparagine/isoleucine), 59 (cysteine/arginine), and 108 (serine/asparagine), all curated target positions.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 1 gives counts and percentages per mutation pattern across 44 samples (e.g. 108-Asn+51-Ile+59-Arg 33 (75.0%); 108-Asn+59-Arg 6 (13.6%)), enabling locus-level frequency extraction.

sub_country_location.evidence: 'Forty-four children... were enrolled in the study in the village of Magoda, Tanga region, Tanzania.' Village-level location given.

collection_window_within_3y.evidence: 'a second therapeutic study was undertaken in 1995' — single year collection window.

african_field_samples.evidence: Field clinical isolates from blood of Tanzanian children in Magoda, Tanga region, Tanzania (laboratory strains used only as controls).

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical therapeutic/molecular field study with patient enrollment, DNA extraction, PCR-RFLP genotyping and results.

duplicate_risk.evidence: 'a second therapeutic study was undertaken in 1995 in the same area among the same population as in 1994' (Rønn et al. 1996), indicating an overlapping/related cohort in the same village; molecular data here are from 1995 samples.

supplement.evidence: All frequency data are in Table 1 in the main text; no supplementary materials are referenced.

assessment.summary: Primary field study of 44 P. falciparum-infected children in Magoda village, Tanga region, Tanzania (sampled 1995). PCR-RFLP genotyping of Pfdhfr codons 51, 59, 108 with extractable mutation frequencies in Table 1. Meets all eligibility criteria; some overlap with a 1994 study in the same population (duplicate risk medium).
